meta.purpose: THE project registry, at system/manifest.json since 2026-07-08 (moved from work/18-recovery-layer, refactor amendment A2) (audit step 3, 2026-07-06): desired-state source of truth for BOTH the Recovery checker (check.ps1) AND the generated surfaces (CLAUDE.md routing table + docs/projects/README.md table + docs/GETTING-STARTED.md + docs/README.md quick start, written by scripts/generate-alex.js, the unified generator since 2026-07-08). Curated by hand: when a project is added, retired, renamed, or changes state/trigger, edit THIS file first, then run the generator. /new writes its entry here before scaffolding. The per-project CLAUDE.md baseline hashes are stored by `check.ps1 -Init` in state/baseline.json (NOT here); the claude_md_sha fields below are reserved/unused. The `hq_project` field is THE authoritative project->Alex-HQ metric-slug map (2026-07-07): work/16-alex-hq/scripts/build-projects.mjs reads it to render the HQ Automation Health board FROM this registry, so every registered project shows (not just the ones that push telemetry). null = the project pushes no live metric (its ticket shows an honest idle state).
meta.states_doc: LIVE = scheduled/event-driven + producing (needs wrapper/error-handler + HQ push). ON-DEMAND = runs when invoked. EVENT = triggered by a detected event, no cron by design. DORMANT = built, waiting on an external dependency (name it + revisit date; two unchanged revisits force activate-or-retire). PARKED = deliberately stopped (resume context + revisit date; same two-strike rule). RETIRED = superseded, tombstoned, strikethrough row. A project may carry LIVE or EVENT for at most 14 days with first_fire null (aging measured from its status.md frontmatter created date); past that the generator V9 warns and check.ps1 C13 goes amber until it fires (a documented drill counts, first_fire_kind=drill) or is re-stated with a reason.
meta.cadence_doc: Upgrade P4 (2026-07-12, design 1.3/MR2-3): `cadence` replaced the old per-day integer field. {expected_hours: N|null, label, note?} - expected_hours is the staleness window HQ/checks age against (null = never stale by age: on-demand/event/dormant/parked/retired); label drives the per-class render rules (design 4.2); note is the human phrase shown on monthly/odd cadences. Multi-job projects are keyed to the tightest job. `first_fire`/`first_fire_kind` (design 1.4/MR2-5): the date a project FIRST produced for real (null = never fired; kind live|drill - a documented drill counts as proof, marked honestly).

known_metric_slugs_no_project.human-actions: the Waiting-on-you queue (upgrade P2, 2026-07-12): system/human-actions.jsonl summary pushed by /alex-hq step 1b; consumed by the P7 HQ strip. Not a project; claimed here so the slug is never mistaken for drift.
known_metric_slugs_no_project.quota: cap-detection metric (upgrade P3, 2026-07-12): pushed by the Pipeline Error Alert on an API-cap hit; mirrored into system/quota-state.json by /alex-hq 3a + brief 4e2. Not a project.

meta.budget_priority_doc: Upgrade P3 (2026-07-12, design 1.7.2): consulted by Test-AlexQuotaGate (scripts/lib/close-out.ps1) ONLY while the Claude PLAN is freshly capped (claude_plan in system/quota-state.json, <=6h old). <=1 = the wrapper still spawns claude -p; absent/default 3 = degraded to deterministic-core-only/skip with an amber PARTIAL push. Kept deliberately dumb (QC over-engineering note 3): an integer, no scheduling logic. Only non-default rows carry the field.
meta.known_work_folders_note: Non-project work/ folders that are legit tooling, not numbered automations. voice = the on-demand two-way voice loop (alex_voice.py; registered as an unnumbered project below). quota-reset-autorun = the P3 quota-cap auto-run tooling (local poller + the n8n 'Quota Reset Auto-Run (one-shot gate)' workflow ardWIfcbe5TwkMm8); registered 2026-07-14 (/deep-audit S1) so it stops flagging as orphan-work. Open sub-item for Shaheen: the documented PersonalOS-qra-poller job is not in Task Scheduler - register via /cron-setup or drop from scheduler/schedule.md if the n8n one-shot supersedes it. C3 allowlists these so a real rogue folder still flags.

unnumbered.name: voice
unnumbered.title: Voice
unnumbered.trigger: every Claude Code session (voice flag + hooks) + Ctrl+Alt+D dictate; v2 loop on-demand
unnumbered.one_liner: Voice v3 'ride the official surface' (research run 22, built 2026-07-12): two-way voice INSIDE the interactive Claude Code session. In: native /voice HOLD dictation (EN/SV, free, review-then-Enter - autoSubmit OFF by design vs acceptEdits) + Ctrl+Alt+D local-whisper dictate lane for AR/SV/EN (types into the prompt, never presses Enter). Out: Stop-hook Edge-TTS->SAPI never-mute speech, gated on outputs/voice/voice-on.flag ('voice on/off' to Alex). $0/mo, no long-lived audio process. v2 open-mic loop (alex_voice.py) stays the on-demand walk-around tool.

Alex proposes; Shaheen decides, except the skills lane self-installs.

projects.name: migration-engine
projects.title: Migration Engine
projects.one_liner: Run a large code/config migration as a dynamic workflow: parallel agents, per-unit self-verification, adversarial parity check, resumable + reversible. Refuses to run without a named target + a verification harness. No target committed yet (P9 dashboard.tsx extraction = the small hand-done precedent).
